title: Evolvulus minimus
intro: Evolvulus minimus, also known as dwarf blue daze or dwarf blue spindrift, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to Mexico and the southern United States. Traditionally, Evolvulus minimus has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Evolvulus minimus include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Evolvulus minimus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to make topical applications for skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Evolvulus minimus is classified as a member of the family Martyniaceae, which is a small family of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Lamiales, which includes a diverse group of plants with similar characteristics. The genus Evolvulus is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dwarf morning glories. Evolvulus minimus is a species within this genus and is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. It is a small, annual herb with small blue or purple flowers.
